title: plagiogyria assurgens
intro: Plagiogyria assurgens is a species of fern, commonly known as the 'taro fern' or 'mokara', native to tropical regions of Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being, with its extracts and preparations believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Plagiogyria assurgens is often used as a decoction or powder, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
